body: ## Bug When using `--capture browser` with the JavaScript CLI on Google Docs URLs, the output loses all images and a significant portion of text content compared to `--capture api`. ## Steps to reproduce ```bash # JS v1.7.6 (internal 0.3.0) web-capture "https://docs.google.com/document/d//edit" --capture api -f archive -o doc-api.zip --engine playwright web-capture "https://docs.google.com/document/d//edit" --capture browser -f archive -o doc-browser.zip --engine playwright ``` ## Expected behavior `--capture browser` should either: 1. Extract meaningful content from the Google Docs page (e.g., via `DOCS_modelChunk` interception as proposed in #72), or 2. Fall back to the export API with a warning, preserving images and content ## Actual behavior `--capture browser` navigates to the `/edit` page, but Google Docs uses canvas-based rendering with no DOM content. The result: - **All images are lost** (0 images vs 30-37 with `--capture api`) - **Content is drastically reduced** (8-11 KB vs 19-23 KB) - **Table structure is lost or degraded** ### Test results (Doc 1 — tables with images) | Capture flag | Lines | Size | Images | |-------------|-------|------|--------| | `--capture api` | 327 | 19,257 bytes | 37 | | `--capture browser` | 290 | 10,947 bytes | **0** | ### Test results (Doc 2 — tables with images and links) | Capture flag | Lines | Size | Images | |-------------|-------|------|--------| | `--capture api` | 10 | 23,359 bytes | 30 | | `--capture browser` | 124 | 8,120 bytes | **0** | ## Tested with - `@link-assistant/web-capture` v1.7.6 (internal 0.3.0) - `--engine playwright` - Two different public Google Docs with tables, images, and links - Archive format ## Root cause Google Docs `/edit` page renders content via `` — there is no HTML DOM with text or `` elements for the browser capture to extract. The JS CLI correctly launches a browser (unlike the Rust version, see #80), but the resulting page has no extractable DOM content. ## Possible solutions 1. **Short-term**: When `--capture browser` detects a Google Docs URL and finds no meaningful DOM content, fall back to `--capture api` with a warning 2. **Long-term**: Implement `DOCS_modelChunk` interception as described in #72 to extract the full document model from the `/edit` page
title: JS: --capture browser on Google Docs drops all images and loses content
